plan.name: topology-real-data-e2e

phase.name: code-cleanup
phase.description: Remove dead code, resolve duplicates, ensure clean baseline

task.title: Consolidate duplicate orchestration stubs
task.description: Root-level orchestration/*.py stubs (analysis_flow.py, dashboard_flow.py) duplicate concepts from orchestration/flows/*.py (the real Prefect flows). The stubs have useful helpers (extract_per_head_metrics, build_topology_comparison, extract_multitask_training_curves, build_topology_comparison_data). Move these into the real flows or a shared module, then delete the stubs.

tdd.red: Test imports: extract_per_head_metrics, build_topology_comparison, extract_multitask_training_curves, build_topology_comparison_data are importable from their new canonical location. Test that orchestration/analysis_flow.py and orchestration/dashboard_flow.py no longer exist (FileNotFoundError in import).
tdd.green: Move helpers to shared module or real flows, delete stubs
tdd.verify: pytest + ruff + grep for broken imports across all test files
tdd.convergence: 3 gates green, no dead stub files

task.acceptance: No duplicate flow files; all imports resolve; 3007+ tests pass

task.title: Audit and test precompute.py
task.description: src/minivess/orchestration/precompute.py provides precompute_auxiliary_targets() using AuxTargetConfig. Verify it works with synthetic data. The module saves .nii.gz format (NIfTI), not .npy — all downstream code must expect NIfTI.

tdd.red: Test precompute_auxiliary_targets() with synthetic 32x32x32 binary mask: (1) SDF output has correct shape, negative inside / positive outside (2) Centreline distance output has correct shape, zero on skeleton (3) Idempotency: skip if output files already exist (4) force=True recomputes even if cached
tdd.green: Fix any broken imports, ensure NIfTI save/load works

task.acceptance: precompute.py tested; SDF + centreline distance verified on synthetic data

phase.name: conditions-runner
phase.description: Extend training to support conditions with wrappers

task.title: Extend run_experiment.py for conditions-based configs
task.description:
Current run_experiment.py iterates `losses` list and delegates to train_monitored.py via importlib CLI. The topology config uses `conditions` with `wrappers`.
Changes needed: 1. Relax REQUIRED_FIELDS: accept either `losses` OR `conditions` (not both required) 2. Detect conditions key in YAML → dispatch to _run_conditions_experiment() 3. _run_conditions_experiment() imports and calls run_fold_safe() directly (NOT via importlib CLI — this is cleaner than adding 10+ CLI args) 4. Per-condition CheckpointManager tracking 5. D2C config propagation to DataConfig

tdd.red: (1) Test conditions YAML with no `losses` key passes load_experiment_config() (2) Test _detect_experiment_mode() returns "conditions" for topology YAML (3) Test _detect_experiment_mode() returns "losses" for legacy YAML (4) Test CheckpointManager.is_condition_complete() tracks per-condition state (5) Test d2c_enabled flag is extracted from condition config
tdd.green: Add _detect_experiment_mode(), _run_conditions_experiment(). Relax REQUIRED_FIELDS to require `experiment_name` and one of `losses`/`conditions`. Import run_fold_safe() as library function (not CLI delegation).
tdd.verify: pytest + ruff + dry-run with topology YAML
tdd.convergence: all 3 gates green; dry-run validates 6 conditions

task.acceptance: `uv run python scripts/run_experiment.py --config configs/experiments/dynunet_topology_all_approaches.yaml --dry-run` validates all 6 conditions without error.

task.title: Build condition-to-model-and-loss factory
task.description:
CRITICAL GLUE CODE: The conditions YAML specifies wrappers (tffm, multitask) and D2C config per condition. Need a factory function that takes: - base_model (DynUNetAdapter instance) - condition_config (dict with wrappers, d2c_enabled, etc.) - loss_name (string, e.g. "cbdice_cldice") And returns: - model (possibly wrapped with TFFMWrapper and/or MultiTaskAdapter) - criterion (possibly MultiTaskLoss wrapping base criterion + aux head losses)
Uses existing apply_wrappers() from model_builder.py. Constructs AuxHeadConfig + AuxHeadLossConfig from YAML auxiliary_heads dicts.

tdd.red: (1) Test build_condition_model() with baseline (no wrappers) returns same model (2) Test build_condition_model() with tffm wrapper returns TFFMWrapper (3) Test build_condition_model() with multitask wrapper returns MultiTaskAdapter (4) Test build_condition_model() with both wrappers returns nested wrapped model (5) Test build_condition_loss() with baseline returns standard criterion (6) Test build_condition_loss() with multitask returns MultiTaskLoss (7) Test AuxHeadConfig constructed from YAML dict has correct fields
tdd.green: Implement build_condition_model(base_model, condition_config) -> nn.Module Implement build_condition_loss(loss_name, condition_config) -> nn.Module Use apply_wrappers() for model wrapping. Construct AuxHeadConfig/AuxHeadLossConfig from YAML auxiliary_heads dicts.
tdd.verify: pytest + ruff + mypy on new module

task.acceptance: Factory correctly builds all 6 condition variants (baseline, d2c_only, multitask, multitask_d2c, tffm, full_pipeline).

task.title: Create debug experiment config for topology
task.description: Create a debug variant of dynunet_topology_all_approaches.yaml with 6 epochs and 3 folds. Named dynunet_topology_all_approaches_debug.yaml to avoid collision with existing dynunet_graph_topology_debug.yaml.

tdd.red: (1) Test debug config loads without error (2) Test has 6 conditions (baseline, d2c_only, multitask, multitask_d2c, tffm, full_pipeline) (3) Test has 6 epochs (not 100) (4) Test has 3 folds (5) Test loss is cbdice_cldice (modeled after test_topology_experiment_config.py)
tdd.green: Create YAML with all 6 conditions, epochs=6, folds=3

task.acceptance: Config loads cleanly; all 6 conditions present with 6-epoch count

task.title: Precompute SDF and centreline distance maps
task.description: Multi-task conditions require SDF and centreline distance GT volumes. Build a precompute script that generates these from binary masks and caches them to data/processed/ as NIfTI (.nii.gz) files. Uses existing precompute_auxiliary_targets() from precompute.py. Uses scipy.ndimage.distance_transform_edt + skimage.morphology.skeletonize_3d (Library-First: same functions already used in centreline_extraction.py).

tdd.red: (1) Test SDF from binary mask: negative inside, positive outside, shape preserved (2) Test centreline distance: zero on skeleton, increases away, shape preserved (3) Test cached file existence check: skip if .nii.gz exists (4) Test CLI script parses --data-dir, --output-dir, --targets args
tdd.green: Implement script wrapping precompute_auxiliary_targets(). Add CLI interface with argparse.
tdd.verify: pytest + run on 1 real volume as smoke test

task.acceptance: `uv run python scripts/precompute_targets.py --data-dir data/raw/minivess --output-dir data/processed --targets sdf,centerline_dist` generates .nii.gz files. Smoke test: 1 volume produces correct shapes.

task.title: Wire precomputed targets into data pipeline

tdd.red: (1) Test DataLoader yields batch with keys: image, label, sdf, centerline_dist when extra_target_keys=["sdf", "centerline_dist"] (2) Test DataLoader yields batch with only image, label when no extra targets (3) Test spatial crops are consistent across all keys (same patch location)
tdd.green: Add extra_target_keys to DataConfig or transform builder. Insert LoadAuxiliaryTargetsd into transform chain. Ensure RandCropByPosNegLabeld keys list includes aux keys.
tdd.verify: pytest + load 1 real volume with targets as integration check

task.acceptance: DataLoader yields batch dict with image, label, sdf, centerline_dist keys. All tensors have consistent spatial dimensions within each batch.

phase.name: debug-training
phase.description: Run actual GPU training with all 6 conditions

task.title: Run precompute on real MiniVess data
task.description: Execute precompute_targets.py to generate SDF and centreline distance maps for all 70 MiniVess volumes. Cache in data/processed/. Verify: load one random file and check shape matches source label.
task.acceptance: 70 x 2 = 140 .nii.gz files in data/processed/. Verification: random file has correct shape matching source label.

task.title: Smoke test: all 6 conditions for 1 epoch
task.description: Before committing to 18 full runs, verify all 6 conditions train for 1 epoch without error. This catches OOM, NaN, and wrapper issues quickly (~20 minutes total). If any condition fails OOM, reduce patch size or skip that condition.
task.acceptance: All 6 conditions complete 1 epoch x 1 fold without OOM or NaN. Peak GPU memory logged per condition. If TFFM or full_pipeline OOM: document in LEARNINGS.md, reduce patch size.

task.title: Execute full debug training (6 conditions x 3 folds x 6 epochs)
task.description: Run the complete debug training using the conditions-based experiment runner. Uses dynunet_topology_all_approaches_debug.yaml (6 epochs, 3 folds). CheckpointManager enables crash-resistant resume. Minimum acceptable: 4/6 conditions complete (if TFFM/full_pipeline OOM).
task.acceptance: At least 4/6 conditions x 3 folds completed. MLflow experiment has 12-18 runs with FINISHED status. Training loss decreases across 6 epochs for all completed conditions. Peak GPU memory under 8 GB. No NaN losses.

phase.name: analysis
phase.description: Generate topology comparison tables and champion models

task.title: Run analysis flow on topology experiment results
task.description: Execute analysis to: - Extract per-fold metrics from training runs - Compute topology comparison table (conditions x 8 metrics) - Run paired bootstrap tests (P1, P3, P4 predictions) - Tag champion models (balanced, topology, overlap) - Generate comparison figures (PNG + SVG) and tables (MD + LaTeX)

tdd.red: (1) Test comparison table has rows for each completed condition (2) Test champion tags written to MLflow (mock) (3) Test output files exist: topology_comparison.md, topology_comparison.tex (4) Test P1/P3/P4 predictions computed
tdd.green: Create analysis script that reads mlruns, builds TopologyComparisonEvaluator, exports tables and figures.
tdd.verify: pytest + run with real mlruns data

task.acceptance: outputs/analysis/topology_comparison.md exists with all completed conditions. outputs/analysis/topology_comparison.tex exists. Champion models tagged in MLflow. P1/P3/P4 prediction results logged.

task.title: MLflow model logging and serving verification
task.description:
Log topology champion as MLflow pyfunc with: - Correct model signature (3D volume input/output) - Wrapper config preserved in model metadata - Loadable via mlflow.pyfunc.load_model() and predict() works
Also verify MLflow serving: load_model + predict on test patch.

tdd.red: (1) Test mlflow.pyfunc.load_model(model_uri) returns a model (2) Test model.predict(input_patch) returns array with shape (1, 2, H, W, D) (3) Test output values in [0, 1] range (softmax probabilities) (4) Test wrapper config preserved in model metadata
tdd.green: Implement model logging with correct signature and metadata

task.acceptance: MLflow pyfunc model loads and predicts correctly. Output is valid segmentation probabilities with correct shape.

phase.name: deploy-serving
phase.description: Export champion to ONNX, verify BentoML serving

task.title: ONNX export from topology champion
task.description: Export topology champion to ONNX. Handle wrapped models: - MultiTaskAdapter: export segmentation head only (strip aux heads) - TFFMWrapper: export if graph ops are ONNX-compatible, else strip - Fallback: export base model only (Risk R3 mitigation)

tdd.red: (1) Test ONNX export produces valid file (onnx.load + onnx.checker.check_model) (2) Test input shape (1, 1, 128, 128, 32), output shape (1, 2, 128, 128, 32) (3) Test file size under 100 MB (4) Test onnxruntime.InferenceSession runs inference on exported model (5) Test MultiTaskAdapter export strips auxiliary heads
tdd.green: Implement export handling for wrapped models

task.acceptance: ONNX model exported, validated with onnxruntime. Input shape: (1, 1, 128, 128, 32). Output: (1, 2, 128, 128, 32). File size under 100 MB.

task.title: BentoML model import and service test
task.description: Import ONNX model into BentoML model store. Verify BentoML service loads and runs inference. Generate bentofile.yaml for deployment.

tdd.red: (1) Test BentoML import succeeds (model in bentoml.models.list()) (2) Test service inference returns valid segmentation output (3) Test bentofile.yaml generated with correct dependencies

task.acceptance: BentoML model imported. Service inference returns valid segmentation. bentofile.yaml generated.

task.title: MONAI Deploy MAP operator test (optional)
task.description: Verify MONAI Deploy MAP operator works with topology champion. Duck-typed operators work without SDK installed. Test operator compute() method with synthetic DataPath.
task.acceptance: MAP operator compute() runs without error on test input

task.title: Run trigger chain: data → (train skipped) → analyze → deploy → dashboard
task.description: Execute run_full_pipeline.py using existing mlruns from T10 (training skipped to avoid redundant GPU time). Verify remaining 4 flows complete: data, analyze, deploy, dashboard. The training flow is deferred to a separate PR (per WARN-08).
task.acceptance: trigger_chain_results.json shows data/analyze/deploy/dashboard as "success". Training "skipped" (by design). Dashboard report generated with topology comparison section.

task.title: Generate paper-ready topology comparison artifacts
task.description: Produce from real training data: - Topology comparison table (LaTeX + Markdown) - Per-condition bar charts - P1/P3/P4 prediction summary - Bootstrap confidence intervals
task.acceptance: outputs/paper_artifacts/ contains topology comparison artifacts. LaTeX table compiles without error. All completed conditions represented in figures.

phase.name: self-review
phase.description: Code review, glitch fixing, PR creation

task.title: Self-review for code quality
task.description: Automated code review checking for: - Dead code / unused imports - Duplicate logic between scripts and flows - Missing error handling in GPU training paths - Consistent MLflow param naming - Type safety in condition/wrapper dispatch Any fixes must follow RED-GREEN-VERIFY: write regression test first.
task.acceptance: All identified glitches fixed; tests still pass

task.title: Create PR with full results
task.description: Create PR with: - Summary of all conditions' training results - Topology comparison table in PR description - Serving verification status (MLflow, BentoML, MONAI Deploy) - Link to trigger chain results JSON

plan.critical_path: T2 → T6 → T7 → T8 (precompute ready) T3, T4, T5 (parallel: conditions infra) T8 + T3 + T4 + T5 → T9 → T10 (training done) T10 → T11 → T13 → T14 (deploy verified) T16 → T17 → T18 → T19 (PR)

parallel_groups.group: T1, T2 can run in parallel
T3, T4, T5 can run in parallel (independent code tasks)
T14, T15 can run in parallel after T13
T12 and T13 can run in parallel after T11

deferred.item: Prefect training flow (Flow 2) — wrap training in @flow decorator and register in trigger chain. Requires significant engineering for config passing through the chain's simple callable interface. Better done after conditions-based training proves stable.

risk.mitigation: Reduce patch size or skip condition. Document in LEARNINGS.md

risks.risk: GPU OOM with TFFM or full_pipeline conditions. Smoke test (T9) catches this early. Min acceptable: 4/6 conditions.
Precompute targets slow for 70 volumes. Each volume is small (512x512x5-110), should be fast (~1 min/volume).
ONNX export fails for wrapped models (TFFMWrapper graph ops). Fallback: strip wrappers and export base DynUNet only.
MONAI Deploy SDK not installed. Duck-typed operators work without SDK.
